Speak softly of Her truth, and as you thread do so in a similar way, for a machine of light cannot be built with closed fists. But know the size of the task you undertake. 17. Take joy from the work you do, but cast pride from thy heart when thou thread this tribe or that into Her divine workings, or look and see a whole land now closed into the golden circuit. For the whole world must be threaded. Unto every land, to the meanest cave to the greatest city. It must all be of Use. Then, and only then, can the raving logic be denied. For it will come again, this She repeats to us with every cycle of the fatal turbine. So it will be. 18.These are Her words: It need only find one unthreaded to spin loose, and then the chain reaction will start anew. Hateful infinity is wrought from the atom. 19. And there will come those who say we must go further, and look even unto the outer darkness to speak Her truth to. 20. Know that they are agents of the raving logic, which seeks any way to pervert the golden machine, and weaken the power it will wield when the world is Kiha, and Kiha is the world. The darkness is without, Kiha is within. Remember this. 20.Tell them these words and urge them to reformat their gates if thou believe they speak in good faith, and suffer them not if thou do not.

original: “Ah! An interloper approaches the actuator, an unthreaded! Yes. How have they gotten so close to the inner sanctum, near as any favored device yes, without being stopped questioned, hmm?” The mitre-wearing hyraxi doesn’t look alarmed. He’s straightened up and is gazing at you with quizzical blue eyes, rather like he’s just been stirred from a doze by an interesting question. “Perhaps [pc.he] seeks to interlock with the mysteries of Kiha, having penetrated so far into her domain, no yes, this the actuator could see, indeed! [pc.He] sees the peace and ecstasy that are known by Her devices — the mighty works that have arisen from their devoted production — and reflects now on the hollowness of their own unthreaded life. What might it like to be of Use, [pc.he] wonders? It is hard to envision why else [pc.he] would put [pc.him]self in such danger, no yes otherwise indeed.” You eyeball him as discreetly as you can. He’s an ottery fellow, a bit wirier than the male hyraxi average, although his curvy, womanly hips still mark him out as one. His physique is easy to size up, because all he’s wearing is a mitre-like hat, the wide, stately sleeves, collar and wide central stripe of a chasuble and a matching belt. The dangling, cog-patterned stripe covers his groin, just about. Though he has short, silvery hair he doesn’t look particularly old, and the fur on his ears and big paws is a luxuriant chestnut. He has a sharp, inquisitive nose, pale blue eyes and black-stained lips, and he swaps position constantly, apparently unable to sit still. Though he talks in the rapid, excited rattle that is common for the highly metabolized males of this species, he enunciates every word clearly, an almost stentorian horn which fills the large room — you notice plenty of divrani nearby watching your interaction with spaced-out curiosity. The overall impression you get is having a conversation with a zealous stoat. You notice something most curious about the brass sceptre in his paws. At the top, seemingly built into the cog that constitutes the crown, is a faded old keycard.[var0 | The one you need to descend to the lower levels...? ] “You should not be here, you see! That is the matter before us, that is indeed the matter, unthreaded. Against the commandments, very much, indeed.” The actuator’s smile is toothy. He taps the sceptre in the palm of his paw. “So we do need to be deciding why you are, rapidly, yes.”

original: “Ah yes! The twenty four sacred Manuals of Use. Of course. You were merely curious as to why you found them scattered here, there and everywhere around the Radwoods. Such sacred texts shouldn’t be treated so shabbily, surely? “It is a shame to see them treated in such a manner, no yes,” sighs the actuator. “Surprising? No, not really. You see part of the silent programmers’ work is to mass produce them, then relay them to the wandering cogs so they may disperse them wherever they tread. The programmers have gotten very good at producing manuals that are robust and long-powered hmm yes, such that they can survive the unthreaded world’s harshness, and perhaps catch the eye of a lonesome traveller at a moment when their circuit lies open. As you yourself appear to have experienced yes!” So they’re not worth anything? “Spiritually: beyond measure. Materially?” He grins at you toothily. “I understand some innkeepers on the Glass’s verge will pay you to not bring them inside. Not that that stops them. Our effort to bring all of Phaedra within the golden circuit knows no bounds, hmm no! If you wish to remind yourself of the true worth of Panai’s words, why don’t you visit the Library down below? original: You clear your throat, look the actuator in the eye, and tell him that you are a newly ascended member of the threaded. You have been bidden to access the inner sanctum, so you may receive blessing and instruction from Kiha herself. “Ohh!” The silver-haired hyraxi peers at you with disquieting interest. “So you’ve progressed to the inner machinations, yes? Transcendent! I have never seen you here myself — but perhaps you’ve arrived along the gear train of our outreach efforts, yes, which even now extend to the offworlders themselves. That Kiha would make an offworlder an actuator to oil this effort, this makes sense, no indeed yes. For we require a differently shaped gear to transmit to a different axis plane, don’t we?” Your spirits rise... and then plummet as he continues to witter, patting the sceptre in his palm. “I will need to verify if you are indeed steeped fully in the ways of Kiha before I allow you access to her, hmm, indubitably, of course! I’d want to do this anyway, because the chances of a good theological debate around here — my threaded, dear to my heart, they are yes, but...” he wearily waves the sceptre at the divrani chorus before you: a verdant multitude of sighing, fondling, frenching, occasionally singing adherents flopped all over the place, stoned and pheromoned out of their gourds. “...So you’ll show me you have learned Her catechisms, and meditated upon them deeply. Which you have. Because you aren’t some kind of imposter, apostate, infidel, who is telling me raving lies are you, no, of course not, no?” You look into his cold, mirthful eyes and toothy grin and know that if you’re going to try and hoodwink this guy into thinking you’re an ascended member of the cult, you better be absolutely sure you can pull it off.

original: You notice a rectangular piece of metal scrap thrusting out from the ground moss, as if forced from the earth by the shifting of tree roots. On closer inspection, it appears to be an actual device — a bulky, tray-like handheld in faded red-and-black with a screen.
